findings.issue: makeRequest timeout surfaces cryptic DOMException message to users. In utils.ts:58, controller.abort() is called without a reason argument. When the 30-second timeout fires, the resulting DOMException propagates through oauthApi.redeem()/init() to UI catch blocks in InvitationCompletePage, OAuthCallbackPage, and InvitationPage, which display e.message directly to users. Chrome produces "signal is aborted without reason", Firefox/Safari produce "The operation was aborted." — neither is actionable for end users.
findings.remediation: Pass a user-friendly reason: controller.abort(new DOMException('Request timed out', 'TimeoutError')). This ensures e.message in downstream catch blocks reads 'Request timed out' instead of the browser default.
